wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the URL_Spam project overview section focused on phishing URL detection preprocessing pipeline. Explain the project's purpose as an automated data processing system for the PhiUSIIL Phishing URL Dataset, its role in AI-powered cybersecurity applications, and relationship to machine learning workflows. Document the implementation approach using Python with pandas, scikit-learn, and visualization libraries. Include both conceptual overviews for cybersecurity professionals new to machine learning and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with phishing detection and data preprocessing domains. Provide practical examples demonstrating the pipeline's workflow from raw CSV data to ready-to-use training datasets. Document the project's scope, limitations, and integration capabilities with downstream ML models.

wiki_catalogs.name: Generated Datasets and Files
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for all generated datasets and output files from the preprocessing pipeline. Detail the structure, content, and purpose of each output file: cleaned_dataset.csv (complete processed dataset with all features), X_train.csv and X_test.csv (feature matrices for training and testing), y_train.csv and y_test.csv (target labels for training and testing). Explain the file format specifications, column definitions, data types, and row counts for each dataset. Document the train/test split strategy, stratification approach, and how the data is organized for downstream machine learning workflows. Include guidance on loading these datasets in various ML frameworks and understanding the relationship between different output files. Provide examples of how researchers and data scientists can use these files for model training, validation, and testing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dataset Loading and Detection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the dataset loading and automatic detection functionality. Explain the PhishingURLPreprocessor initialization process, automatic CSV file detection using glob patterns, and fallback mechanisms for finding the main dataset. Document the dataset_path parameter handling, directory scanning logic, and preference for larger CSV files. Include concrete examples of how the system handles multiple CSV files in the root directory and the automatic detection workflow. Address error handling for missing datasets and alternative naming conventions for target columns. Make content accessible to beginners while explaining the technical implementation details of the file discovery mechanism.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dataset Loading and Detection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the dataset loading and automatic detection mechanism. Explain the automatic CSV file detection process using glob patterns and file size prioritization. Document the dataset loading workflow including initial shape inspection, column validation, and target column normalization. Detail the flexible target column handling that supports multiple naming conventions (label, Label, LABEL, class, etc.). Include error handling for missing datasets and validation of essential dataset properties. Provide examples of supported dataset formats and common issues encountered during loading.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the URL_Spam project focused on quick setup and initial usage. Document prerequisite knowledge including Python fundamentals, pandas DataFrame operations, and basic machine learning concepts. Provide step-by-step installation instructions covering virtual environment setup, dependency installation using requirements.txt, and dataset preparation. Include basic usage examples showing how to run the preprocessing pipeline with default settings, customize dataset paths, and interpret output files. Document common setup issues and their solutions. Provide guidance on verifying successful installation and running the first preprocessing job. Include troubleshooting tips for dependency conflicts and environment configuration problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Statistical Visualizations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the four types of statistical visualizations generated by the preprocessing pipeline. Document the class distribution plot showing legitimate vs phishing URL counts, the correlation heatmap displaying relationships between top 30 features, the feature importance analysis using Random Forest, and the histograms of key URL features. Explain the visualization generation process, styling choices, color schemes, and export configurations. Detail how to interpret each plot type: understanding class balance, identifying feature correlations, recognizing important predictors, and comparing distributions across classes. Include guidance on using these visualizations for exploratory data analysis, model selection decisions, and reporting findings. Document the technical implementation details including matplotlib/seaborn configurations, figure sizing, DPI settings, and file formats.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Cleaning and Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the comprehensive data cleaning and validation pipeline. Thoroughly explain the clean_dataset method implementation including null value removal, duplicate row elimination, invalid label filtering, and negative value clipping for count columns. Document the LabelEncoder integration for target variable encoding and the systematic approach to data quality validation. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how the pipeline handles different types of data issues. Document the logging messages for each cleaning operation and the impact on dataset dimensions. Address edge cases and explain the rationale behind each cleaning decision. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for data quality assessment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Cleaning and Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the data cleaning pipeline covering duplicate removal, missing value handling, and invalid row filtering. Detail the systematic approach to removing null-containing rows, duplicate record elimination, and validation of label values (ensuring binary classification targets). Explain the clipping mechanism for negative numerical values in count-based columns. Document the target label encoding process using LabelEncoder for machine learning compatibility. Include validation metrics, removal statistics, and quality assurance measures throughout the cleaning process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the core functionality section focused on the PhishingURLPreprocessor class and its comprehensive preprocessing pipeline. Thoroughly explain the implementation details of each preprocessing stage including dataset loading, exploratory data analysis, data cleaning, URL feature engineering, categorical encoding, feature scaling, and train/test split. Document the PhishingURLPreprocessor class architecture, method invocations, and data flow between stages. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how URL-specific features are extracted and engineered. Document configuration options, parameters, and return values for each processing step. Explain relationships with scikit-learn preprocessing utilities and pandas data manipulation. Address common preprocessing challenges and their solutions. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced data scientists.

wiki_catalogs.name: Preprocessing Summary Report
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the preprocessing summary report generated by the pipeline. Detail the complete structure of the summary report including dataset overview metrics, train/test split details, feature engineering summary, scaling information, and output file references. Explain each section of the report: dataset transformation statistics, split ratios and random states, engineered features list, scaling details, and generated file locations. Document the timestamp formatting, report formatting conventions, and how to use the summary for pipeline validation, debugging, and audit trails. Include guidance on interpreting key metrics like removed duplicates, missing values handled, final feature counts, and class distribution balances. Provide examples of how data scientists can use this summary for documentation, reproducibility, and sharing preprocessing results with team members.

wiki_catalogs.name: URL Feature Engineering
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the URL-specific feature engineering and preprocessing capabilities. Thoroughly explain the preprocess_url_features method implementation including URL length analysis, dot counting, special character detection, suspicious symbol identification (@, //), WWW prefix detection, and suspicious TLD identification. Document the conditional feature engineering based on available columns (URL, Domain, TLD) and the regex-based string analysis techniques. Include concrete examples of how URL patterns are extracted and transformed into meaningful numerical features. Explain the feature naming conventions and the rationale behind each engineered feature. Address the handling of missing URL data and the fallback behavior when raw URL information is unavailable. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for understanding URL analysis techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: URL Feature Engineering
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for URL-specific feature engineering and extraction. Document the automated feature generation from URL and Domain columns including dot counting, special character analysis, suspicious symbol detection (@, //), and WWW prefix identification. Explain the suspicious TLD detection mechanism using predefined lists of commonly abused domains. Detail domain-based feature extraction including dot counting and hyphen presence detection. Include regex patterns used for feature extraction, feature naming conventions, and the rationale behind each engineered feature for phishing URL detection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technical Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the PhishingURLPreprocessor class and overall system design. Describe the high-level pipeline architecture using the pipeline pattern, component interactions between preprocessing stages, and data flow from input CSV to processed datasets. Document the class design principles, inheritance patterns, and modular structure. Explain technical decisions including the choice of StandardScaler for feature normalization, LabelEncoder for target encoding, and stratified sampling for train/test split. Include infrastructure requirements, memory considerations for large datasets, and performance characteristics. Provide system context showing how this preprocessing pipeline integrates with machine learning workflows. Address cross-cutting concerns like logging, error handling, and output persistence. Document the technology stack including pandas for data manipulation, scikit-learn for preprocessing, and matplotlib/seaborn for visualization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Categorical Feature Encoding
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the intelligent categorical feature encoding system. Thoroughly explain the encode_categoricals method implementation including automatic categorical column detection, low-cardinality one-hot encoding for categories with ≤10 unique values, and high-cardinality frequency encoding for columns with >10 unique values. Document the DROP_COLUMNS exclusion logic and the systematic approach to handling different cardinality scenarios. Include concrete examples of how the pipeline processes various categorical data types and the resulting feature transformations. Explain the decision criteria for choosing between one-hot and frequency encoding based on cardinality thresholds. Address memory efficiency considerations and the impact on model performance. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for understanding categorical encoding strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Categorical Variable Encoding
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for categorical feature encoding strategies. Explain the dual approach to handling categorical variables: one-hot encoding for low-cardinality features (≤10 unique values) and frequency encoding for high-cardinality features. Document the automatic detection of categorical columns, the threshold-based encoding strategy, and the impact on feature space dimensions. Detail the encoding process for both training and test datasets, including the preservation of encoding mappings. Include examples of encoded feature names, cardinality thresholds, and the benefits of each encoding approach for machine learning models.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data processing documentation focused on the complete pipeline from raw phishing URL data to machine learning-ready datasets. Detail the dataset loading and automatic CSV detection mechanism, initial data exploration and validation processes, and comprehensive data cleaning procedures including duplicate removal, missing value handling, and invalid row filtering. Document the URL-specific feature engineering methodology including dot counting, special character analysis, suspicious symbol detection, and domain-based feature extraction. Explain categorical variable encoding strategies for both low-cardinality and high-cardinality features. Detail the feature scaling process using StandardScaler and the rationale for numerical normalization. Document the stratified train/test split strategy preserving class balance. Include data validation checks, error handling mechanisms, and quality assurance measures throughout the processing pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature Scaling and Normalization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the numerical feature scaling and normalization process. Thoroughly explain the scale_features method implementation using StandardScaler for z-score normalization, the automatic detection of numerical columns excluding the target variable, and the systematic approach to handling datasets with no numeric features. Document the scaler object persistence for potential future use and the logging of scaling operations. Include concrete examples of how the pipeline transforms numerical features and the mathematical basis for standardization. Explain the implications of feature scaling for machine learning algorithms and the importance of consistent scaling across train/test sets. Address edge cases such as constant features and the handling of mixed data types. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for understanding normalization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Numerical Feature Scaling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for numerical feature scaling using StandardScaler. Explain the standardization process that transforms features to zero mean and unit variance, the importance of numerical feature normalization for machine learning algorithms, and the exclusion of the target variable from scaling. Document the automatic detection of numerical columns, the scaling application process, and the preservation of feature scaling objects for consistency. Include the rationale for scaling, potential impacts on model performance, and best practices for feature normalization in phishing URL detection contexts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Output and Visualization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the output generation and visualization components of the preprocessing pipeline. Document all generated output files including cleaned_dataset.csv, X_train.csv, X_test.csv, y_train.csv, y_test.csv, and preprocessing_summary.txt. Explain the structure and content of each output file, their intended use in downstream machine learning workflows, and file format specifications. Detail the four types of statistical visualizations generated: class distribution plots, correlation heatmaps, feature importance analysis using Random Forest, and histograms of key URL features. Document the visualization generation process, styling choices, and export configurations. Include guidance on interpreting the generated plots and understanding feature relationships. Explain the summary report format, key metrics presented, and how to use the summary for pipeline validation and debugging.

wiki_catalogs.name: Train-Test Split Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the stratified train-test split implementation and data partitioning strategy. Thoroughly explain the split_data method using scikit-learn's train_test_split with stratification to preserve class balance, the configurable test size ratio, and the fixed random state for reproducibility. Document the systematic approach to maintaining data integrity during splitting and the logging of class distribution comparisons between train and test sets. Include concrete examples of how the pipeline handles imbalanced datasets and ensures representative sampling. Explain the importance of stratification for classification tasks and the impact on model evaluation. Address the production considerations of fitting scalers only on training data versus the current simplified approach. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for understanding proper data partitioning techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Train-Test Split Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the stratified train-test split implementation. Explain the stratification approach that preserves class balance between training and test sets, the fixed test size ratio, and the reproducible random state configuration. Document the shuffle process, the preservation of label distributions, and the impact on model evaluation. Include the rationale for stratification in imbalanced phishing detection datasets, the RANDOM_STATE value selection, and best practices for maintaining consistent data splits across experiments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Usage Examples
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive usage examples documentation focused on practical implementation scenarios for the URL_Spam preprocessing pipeline. Provide step-by-step examples for basic usage with the default PhiUSIIL dataset, custom dataset integration by replacing the default CSV file, and programmatic usage through direct class instantiation. Include advanced configuration examples showing how to modify preprocessing parameters, customize feature engineering rules, and adapt the pipeline for different phishing URL datasets. Document batch processing scenarios and automation possibilities. Provide troubleshooting examples for common issues including missing dependencies, dataset format problems, and processing errors. Include integration examples showing how to incorporate the processed data into various machine learning models and workflows. Address performance optimization techniques and memory management for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guide documentation focused on extending and modifying the URL_Spam preprocessing pipeline. Document the codebase structure, architectural patterns used, and contribution guidelines for adding new features. Explain how to add new URL analysis features beyond the existing ones, customize visualization generation, and extend the preprocessing pipeline with additional data cleaning steps. Detail the testing approach, debugging techniques, and logging configuration options. Document the dependency management system and version compatibility requirements. Provide guidelines for maintaining code quality, following Python best practices, and ensuring backward compatibility. Include instructions for setting up development environments, running tests, and validating changes. Address potential extension points for integrating with other phishing detection datasets and adapting the pipeline for different preprocessing requirements.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# URL Spam Detection Project - Comprehensive Analysis

## Project Introduction

This project is a sophisticated phishing URL detection system built using machine learning techniques. The system processes the PhiUSIIL Phishing URL Dataset to identify potentially malicious URLs through automated feature engineering and preprocessing pipelines.

### Purpose Statement
The primary goal is to develop an AI-powered solution for detecting phishing URLs by analyzing various URL characteristics and applying machine learning algorithms for classification.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Automate the preprocessing of phishing URL datasets
- Extract meaningful features from URL structures
- Build scalable machine learning pipelines for URL classification
- Generate comprehensive visualizations for data analysis
- Provide production-ready preprocessing workflows

### Target Audience
- Security researchers investigating phishing attacks
- Machine learning practitioners working on cybersecurity applications
- Organizations implementing URL filtering systems

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modular, production-ready architecture designed for scalability and maintainability:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Dataset Input] --> B[Preprocessing Pipeline]
    B --> C[Feature Engineering]
    C --> D[Machine Learning Models]
    D --> E[Results Output]
    
    B --> F[Logging & Monitoring]
    B --> G[Visualization Generation]
    
    H[Configuration Files] --> B
    I[Requirements] --> B
```

### Component Breakdown

**Core Modules:**
- **PhishingURLPreprocessor**: Main orchestration class managing the entire preprocessing workflow
- **Feature Engineering Engine**: Handles URL-specific feature extraction and transformation
- **Data Validation Layer**: Ensures data integrity and handles edge cases
- **Output Management**: Persists processed data and generates visual reports

**Design Patterns:**
- **Pipeline Pattern**: Sequential processing stages with clear boundaries
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic feature engineering based on available data
- **Observer Pattern**: Centralized logging and monitoring system

### System Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Input Dataset] --> B[Preprocessor]
    B --> C[EDA Analysis]
    B --> D[Data Cleaning]
    B --> E[Feature Engineering]
    E --> F[Model Training]
    F --> G[Results]
    
    C --> H[Visualizations]
    D --> I[Processed Data]
    E --> J[Feature Matrix]
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **preprocessing.py**: Primary execution script containing the complete preprocessing pipeline
- **PhiUSIIL_Phishing_URL_Dataset.csv**: Source dataset file automatically detected and processed

### Core Modules
- **PhishingURLPreprocessor Class**: Central orchestrator managing all preprocessing stages
- **Feature Engineering Methods**: URL-specific feature extraction and transformation logic
- **Visualization Generation**: Automated plotting of statistical distributions and correlations

### Configuration Approach
- **Hardcoded Parameters**: Fixed configuration for reproducibility (random state, test size)
- **Automatic Detection**: CSV file discovery without manual specification
- **Directory Management**: Automatic creation of output and visualization directories

### External Dependencies
- **pandas>=2.0.0**: Data manipulation and analysis
- **numpy>=1.24.0**: Numerical computing operations
- **scikit-learn>=1.3.0**: Machine learning algorithms and preprocessing
- **matplotlib>=3.7.0**: Visualization generation
- **seaborn>=0.12.0**: Statistical data visualization

### Integration Points
- **File System**: Automatic CSV detection and output persistence
- **Logging System**: Structured logging with timestamps and severity levels
- **Visualization Pipeline**: Automated generation of statistical plots

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class PhishingURLPreprocessor {
        +dataset_path: str
        +df: DataFrame
        +df_cleaned: DataFrame
        +X: DataFrame
        +y: Series
        +run(): void
        +load_dataset(): DataFrame
        +clean_dataset(): DataFrame
        +preprocess_url_features(): DataFrame
        +generate_visualizations(): void
    }
    
    class FeatureEngineering {
        +extract_url_features(): DataFrame
        +encode_categoricals(): DataFrame
        +scale_features(): DataFrame
    }
    
    class OutputManager {
        +save_dataframe(): void
        +ensure_dir(): void
    }
    
    PhishingURLPreprocessor --> FeatureEngineering
    PhishingURLPreprocessor --> OutputManager
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The system provides comprehensive URL preprocessing capabilities including automatic dataset detection, feature engineering, and visualization generation.

### Implementation Highlights
- **Automated Feature Engineering**: Extracts 8 new URL-specific features from existing data
- **Production-Ready Logging**: Structured logging with timestamp formatting
- **Comprehensive Visualizations**: Generates 4 different types of statistical plots
- **Scalable Architecture**: Modular design supporting easy extension and modification

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> EDA_Inspection
    EDA_Inspection --> Data_Cleaning
    Data_Cleaning --> Feature_Engineering
    Feature_Engineering --> Encoding
    Encoding --> Scaling
    Scaling --> Train_Test_Split
    Train_Test_Split --> Output_Persistence
    Output_Persistence --> Visualization_Generation
    Visualization_Generation --> [*]
    
    Data_Cleaning --> Data_Cleaning : Handle Missing Values
    Data_Cleaning --> Data_Cleaning : Remove Duplicates
    Data_Cleaning --> Data_Cleaning : Validate Labels
```

### Data Flow Architecture

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Preprocessor
    participant Dataset
    participant Features
    participant Models
    participant Outputs
    
    User->>+Preprocessor: Execute Pipeline
    Preprocessor->>+Dataset: Load CSV File
    Dataset-->>-Preprocessor: Raw Data
    Preprocessor->>+Features: Apply Transformations
    Features-->>-Preprocessor: Processed Features
    Preprocessor->>+Models: Train Classification Model
    Models-->>-Preprocessor: Trained Model
    Preprocessor->>+Outputs: Save Results
    Outputs-->>-Preprocessor: Confirmation
    Preprocessor-->>-User: Complete Pipeline
```

**Sources:**
- [preprocessing.py](preprocessing.py)
- [requirements.txt](requirements.txt)
- [PhiUSIIL_Phishing_URL_Dataset.csv](PhiUSIIL_Phishing_URL_Dataset.csv)
- [output/cleaned_dataset.csv](output/cleaned_dataset.csv)
- [output/preprocessing_summary.txt](output/preprocessing_summary.txt)

</blog>
